text: Marathon performance can be predicted from 10-km race time, body mass index, age, and sex using machine learning algorithms with very high correlation to actual performance (r>0.90).
K-nearest neighbor (KNN) algorithm predicts marathon time from 10-km performance with a mean absolute error of 2.4%, outperforming artificial neural networks.
Artificial neural network (ANN) algorithm predicts marathon time from 10-km performance with a mean absolute error of 5.6%.
10-km race time is a valid input variable for predicting marathon performance, when combined with body mass index, age, and sex.
AI-predicted marathon performances show no statistically significant difference from actual marathon performances (p>0.05), supporting their use in training program planning.
